value.market_read: WTI Curve: WTI settled at $76.54 in the prior session, lifted by the latest WPSR (week ending June 12, released Jun 17) which reported a prompt draw that reinforced tightening Cushing balances. The reported draw drove front-month gains and pulled the M1 contract further above deferred months, sharpening the existing backwardation. The curve descends monotonically from $76.54 (Aug-26) to $70.00 (Jun-27), an M1–M12 spread of $6.54 that signals genuine prompt tightness rather than a storage-economics surplus. The Aug–Sep (M1–M2) spread of roughly $0.92 in backwardation underlines that the market is bidding up immediately available barrels. Watch: Whether subsequent Cushing draws sustain the prompt premium or whether deferred-month buying flattens the curve. Brent Curve: Brent settled at $80.59, also supported by the post-WPSR tone, holding a healthy premium to WTI. The curve is backwardated from $80.59 (Aug-26) to $74.22 (Jun-27), an M1–M12 of $6.37 — slightly shallower than WTI's, consistent with global prompt tightness but a touch less acute at the very front than the Cushing-driven US picture. The Brent backwardation reflects firm waterborne demand and OPEC+ supply discipline rather than a storage glut. The near-dated structure remains firmly inverted, a classic signal of supply tightness near-term. Watch: Hormuz/geopolitical risk premium and whether OPEC+ barrels loosen the prompt structure into Q3. RBOB Gasoline Curve: RBOB settled at $2.9122, with the prompt firmly bid as peak summer driving demand pulls on gasoline supply. The curve is steeply backwardated through the summer strip — $2.9122 (Aug-26) falling to a $2.2771 trough around Feb-27 before kicking back up to ~$2.49 as the 2027 summer spec re-enters — an M1–M12 of $0.4268 that screams seasonal prompt tightness. That summer-to-winter collapse of roughly 60+ cents is the textbook shape: the market prices peak-season tightness now and surplus into the shoulder/winter months. The front-spread backwardation is the steepest of the complex on a percentage basis, reflecting the summer demand pull. Watch: Gasoline crack strength and refinery run rates into the back half of driving season — any demand disappointment unwinds the prompt premium fast. ULSD Diesel Curve: ULSD settled at $3.1508, supported by firm distillate balances and the broadly constructive post-WPSR tone. The curve is smoothly backwardated from $3.1508 (Aug-26) down to $2.7935 (Jun-27), an M1–M12 of $0.3573 — a steadier, less seasonally whipsawed shape than gasoline, reflecting distillate's year-round industrial and freight demand. The orderly descent signals prompt tightness without the sharp summer-peak inversion seen in RBOB. The M1–M2 spread near $0.034 in backwardation points to firm but not panicked prompt demand. Watch: Diesel cracks and the diesel/jet regrade heading toward early-winter heating-demand build.
value.kpi_wti: WTI settled at $76.54, the prior-session front-month close, firmed by the latest WPSR draw and tightening Cushing balances.
value.kpi_brent: Brent settled at $80.59, holding a firm premium to WTI and a backwardated structure consistent with global prompt tightness.
value.kpi_rbob: RBOB settled at $2.9122, with the prompt bid hard by peak summer driving-season gasoline demand.
value.kpi_ulsd: ULSD settled at $3.1508, supported by firm year-round distillate demand and a steady backwardated curve.
value.kpi_bw: The Brent–WTI spread at $4.05 is relatively narrow, signaling weak/uneconomic US export arb — barrels are more likely to stay domestic, and re-widening (via WTI falling or Brent rising) would be needed to restore export competitiveness.
value.kpi_curve_regime: All four contracts are in backwardation, with WTI ($6.54 M1–M12) and RBOB (seasonally steep) signaling firm prompt tightness rather than a contango storage surplus.
